facts.text: Swan Lake, composed by Tchaikovsky, is one of the most performed ballets in history and tells the story of Princess Odette, cursed to live as a swan by day.
Mikhail Baryshnikov defected from the Soviet Union in 1974 while touring with the Kirov Ballet in Toronto, Canada.
The five basic positions of the feet in ballet were codified by Pierre Beauchamp in the late 17th century and remain the foundation of all ballet technique.
Martha Graham is often called the mother of modern dance and developed a technique based on contraction and release of the torso.
A professional ballerina can go through 2 to 3 pairs of pointe shoes per week during heavy performance schedules.
The Nutcracker was first performed at the Mariinsky Theatre in St. Petersburg, Russia.
The Bolshoi Ballet, founded in 1776, is one of the oldest and most prestigious ballet companies in the world.
Breakdancing, also known as breaking, was added as an Olympic sport for the 2024 Paris Games.
An arabesque is a position where the dancer stands on one leg with the other extended behind, creating a long line from fingertips to toes.
The film Black Swan (2010) starred Natalie Portman, who trained for a year to convincingly portray a professional ballerina.
Louis XIV of France, known as the Sun King, was an avid dancer and helped establish ballet as a formal art form in the 17th century.
Alvin Ailey founded the Alvin Ailey American Dance Theater in New York City in 1958 to celebrate African American culture through modern dance.
Pointe shoes are traditionally made with layers of burlap, paper, and fabric hardened with paste, not wood as commonly believed.
George Balanchine co-founded the New York City Ballet in 1948 and is considered the father of American ballet.
The grand jeté is a dramatic leap where the dancer appears to do a split in midair.
Pina Bausch revolutionized dance theater (Tanztheater) by blending movement, speech, and theatrical staging in her works.
Capezio, one of the oldest dance shoe companies, was founded in 1887 by Salvatore Capezio near the Metropolitan Opera House in New York.
The Vaganova method, developed by Agrippina Vaganova in the 1930s, is the dominant ballet training system used in Russia.
Dirty Dancing (1987) became a cultural phenomenon and popularized the phrase 'Nobody puts Baby in a corner.'
A ballet dancer's turnout—rotating the legs outward from the hips—is essential to classical technique and takes years to develop.
Anna Pavlova's famous solo, The Dying Swan, was choreographed for her by Michel Fokine in 1905.
The Royal Academy of Dance (RAD) was founded in London in 1920 and is one of the largest dance education organizations in the world.
Misty Copeland became the first African American woman to be promoted to principal dancer at American Ballet Theatre in 2015.
A plié, meaning 'to bend,' is the most fundamental movement in ballet and is used to warm up muscles and prepare for jumps.
Riverdance debuted as a seven-minute interval act during the 1994 Eurovision Song Contest and became a global sensation.
The Paris Opera Ballet, founded in 1669, is the oldest professional ballet company still active today.
Jerome Robbins choreographed the dance sequences in West Side Story, blending ballet with jazz and Latin dance.
Flamenco originated in the Andalusia region of Spain and combines singing, guitar, dance, and handclaps.
Ballet originated in the Italian Renaissance courts of the 15th century before being developed further in France.
The Mariinsky Ballet, formerly the Kirov, is based in St. Petersburg and has produced many of the world's greatest dancers.
Rudolf Nureyev defected from the Soviet Union in 1961 at Le Bourget Airport in Paris, an event known as the 'leap to freedom.'
A professional ballet dancer typically retires between the ages of 35 and 40 due to the extreme physical demands on the body.
Twyla Tharp is known for her crossover works that blend ballet, modern dance, and popular music.
The ballet Giselle, premiered in 1841, is considered one of the great Romantic ballets and features supernatural beings called Wilis.
Tap dance evolved from a fusion of African rhythmic stomping and Irish jig dancing in 19th-century America.
Marius Petipa choreographed over 50 ballets, including the definitive versions of Swan Lake, The Sleeping Beauty, and The Nutcracker.
Billy Elliot (2000) tells the story of a young boy in northern England who discovers a passion for ballet against his family's wishes.
A tendu (meaning 'stretched') is a movement where the working foot slides along the floor until only the tip of the toe remains in contact.
Carlos Acosta, born in Havana, Cuba, became one of the most celebrated male dancers of his generation and a principal at the Royal Ballet.
The Sleeping Beauty, with music by Tchaikovsky and choreography by Petipa, premiered at the Mariinsky Theatre in 1890.
International Dance Day is celebrated every year on April 29, the birthday of Jean-Georges Noverre, the creator of modern ballet.
Merce Cunningham pioneered the idea that dance and music could be created independently and combined in performance.
The Bolshoi Theatre in Moscow underwent a massive six-year renovation and reopened in 2011.
En pointe dancing, where a ballerina supports her entire body weight on the tips of her toes, was developed in the early 19th century.
So You Think You Can Dance premiered on Fox in 2005 and introduced millions of viewers to contemporary and ballroom dance styles.
A pirouette is a controlled turn on one leg, and advanced dancers can execute multiple pirouettes in succession.
Freed of London has been making pointe shoes by hand since 1929 and supplies many of the world's top ballet companies.
Kenneth MacMillan's Romeo and Juliet, created for the Royal Ballet in 1965, is one of the most popular full-length ballets in the repertoire.
Hip-hop dance originated in the 1970s in New York City's South Bronx alongside the hip-hop music movement.
La Bayadère, set in India, features the famous 'Kingdom of the Shades' scene where 32 dancers descend a ramp in perfect unison.
Margot Fonteyn and Rudolf Nureyev formed one of the most legendary partnerships in ballet history, dancing together from 1962 until the 1970s.
The waltz was considered scandalous when it was first introduced to European ballrooms in the 18th century because partners held each other closely.
William Forsythe is known for deconstructing classical ballet and pushing its boundaries with works like In the Middle, Somewhat Elevated.
Center Stage (2000) is a cult favorite film that follows young dancers at a fictional version of the American Ballet Theatre's school.
Ballet dancers can exert force on their toes equal to several times their body weight during jumps and landings.
Don Quixote, based on Cervantes' novel, is known for its virtuosic solos and lively Spanish-flavored choreography.
Agrippina Vaganova published her influential textbook 'Basic Principles of Classical Ballet' in 1934.
The Joffrey Ballet, founded in 1956 by Robert Joffrey, is known for producing innovative and accessible ballet.
Jazz dance evolved from African American vernacular dance and was popularized in Broadway musicals and Hollywood films.
A ballet barre is used for warm-up exercises and balance support, not as a prop—dancers must eventually perform all movements without it.
Dancing with the Stars (based on the UK's Strictly Come Dancing) has been one of the most popular reality TV shows since 2005.
Tchaikovsky composed the music for three of the most famous ballets ever: Swan Lake, The Sleeping Beauty, and The Nutcracker.
The Romantic era of ballet (1830s–1850s) introduced themes of the supernatural, with ballerinas dancing en pointe to appear otherworldly.
Coppélia, first performed in 1870, is a comedic ballet about a man who falls in love with a life-sized dancing doll.
Michael Flatley's Lord of the Dance showcased Irish step dancing to audiences worldwide and broke box office records.
The pas de deux (step of two) is a dance for two people, typically a ballerina and a male partner, and is the centerpiece of many classical ballets.
Anna Pavlova was born in St. Petersburg, Russia, and became one of the most famous ballerinas in history.
Bloch, founded in 1932 by Jacob Bloch in Sydney, Australia, is one of the world's leading producers of dance shoes and apparel.
The Step Up film franchise (2006–2019) combined street dance and hip-hop with romance and helped popularize urban dance styles.
A battement is a beating action of the legs and comes in many varieties including frappé, fondu, and grand battement.
Alvin Ailey's signature work, Revelations (1960), set to African American spirituals, is one of the most performed modern dance pieces ever.
The English National Ballet, founded in 1950 by Alicia Markova and Anton Dolin, tours extensively throughout the UK.
Ballroom dancing includes styles such as the foxtrot, tango, Viennese waltz, quickstep, and slow waltz.
George Balanchine was born Giorgi Balanchivadze in Tbilisi, Georgia, and trained at the Imperial Ballet School in St. Petersburg.
Fonteyn was made a Dame Commander of the Order of the British Empire in 1956 for her contributions to ballet.
A chassé is a gliding step where one foot 'chases' the other, commonly used as a linking movement in ballet combinations.
The American Ballet Theatre (ABT), founded in 1940, is one of the three leading classical ballet companies in the United States.
Isadora Duncan is considered a pioneer of modern dance, rejecting rigid ballet technique in favor of free, natural movement.
The fouetté turn involves a whipping motion of the working leg and is one of the most technically demanding ballet steps.
In the Black Swan pas de deux, the ballerina traditionally performs 32 consecutive fouetté turns.
Martha Graham's company, the Martha Graham Dance Company, is the oldest continuously performing modern dance company in America.
Fred Astaire, though primarily a film star, was trained in ballroom and tap and helped elevate dance in Hollywood to an art form.
The corps de ballet refers to the group of dancers who perform together as an ensemble, as opposed to soloists and principal dancers.
Mikhail Baryshnikov was born in Riga, Latvia, and trained at the Vaganova Academy in Leningrad.
The tango originated in the working-class neighborhoods of Buenos Aires, Argentina, in the late 19th century.
Pointe shoes typically last only 10 to 20 hours of dancing before they need to be replaced.
Flashdance (1983) inspired a generation of dancers with its iconic scenes of a welder who dreams of attending a prestigious dance conservatory.
A relevé is the action of rising from a flat foot to the ball of the foot or full pointe.
The San Francisco Ballet, founded in 1933, is the oldest professional ballet company in America.
Rudolf Nureyev was born on a train near Irkutsk, Siberia, while his mother was traveling on the Trans-Siberian Railway.
Balanchine's Serenade, set to Tchaikovsky's Serenade for Strings, was the first ballet he created in America.
Breakdancing (breaking) includes four main elements: toprock, downrock, power moves, and freezes.
The Cecchetti method of ballet training, developed by Italian ballet master Enrico Cecchetti, emphasizes musicality and clean lines.
Ginger Rogers famously did everything Fred Astaire did, but backwards and in high heels.
An entrechat is a jump in which the dancer's legs are rapidly crossed and uncrossed in the air.
The Stuttgart Ballet in Germany gained international fame under the direction of choreographer John Cranko in the 1960s and 1970s.
Swan Lake premiered at the Bolshoi Theatre in Moscow in 1877, but was initially considered a failure.
Savion Glover revitalized tap dance in the 1990s with his show Bring in 'da Noise, Bring in 'da Funk.
A demi-plié is a half bend of the knees, while a grand plié is a full bend where the thighs become parallel to the floor.
Pina Bausch's Tanztheater Wuppertal performed her works for over 40 years and toured worldwide until her death in 2009.
The ballet flat shoe, worn in everyday fashion, was inspired by the soft practice shoes worn by ballet dancers.
Marie Taglioni is credited with being the first ballerina to dance a full ballet en pointe, in La Sylphide in 1832.
Contemporary dance is a genre that combines elements of several dance styles including modern, jazz, lyrical, and classical ballet.
The Dance Theatre of Harlem, founded by Arthur Mitchell in 1969, was the first Black classical ballet company.
A glissade is a gliding step that travels along the floor and is often used as a preparatory step before jumps.
The Kirov Ballet (now Mariinsky) produced legends like Nureyev, Baryshnikov, Natalia Makarova, and Diana Vishneva.
Bob Fosse's distinctive choreographic style—turned-in knees, rolled shoulders, jazz hands—defined Broadway dance for decades.
Professional ballet dancers eat between 2,000 and 3,000 calories daily to fuel their intense training schedules.
Misty Copeland was born in Kansas City, Missouri, and didn't begin ballet training until age 13, which is considered very late.
The adagio section of a ballet class focuses on slow, controlled movements that develop balance, extension, and musicality.
Flamenco dancing is characterized by emotional intensity, proud posture, expressive arm movements, and rhythmic footwork called zapateado.
Le Sacre du Printemps (The Rite of Spring) by Nijinsky caused a riot at its 1913 Paris premiere due to its radical choreography.
The Australian Ballet, founded in 1962, is based in Melbourne and is the country's largest classical dance company.
Gene Kelly brought a more athletic, masculine style of dance to Hollywood musicals, most famously in Singin' in the Rain (1952).
A développé is a movement where the working leg is drawn up to the knee and then extended outward in a slow, controlled motion.
The Bournonville style, developed by August Bournonville in Denmark, emphasizes lightness, ballon, and joy in movement.
Josephine Baker was an American-born French entertainer who became one of the most celebrated performers in Europe during the 1920s.
Rosin is applied to the soles of pointe shoes to prevent slipping on stage, while some dancers also rosin inside their shoes for grip.
The Juilliard School in New York City offers one of the most prestigious dance training programs in the world.
Irish step dancing is characterized by a rigid upper body and rapid, intricate footwork.
An attitude is a position where one leg is raised behind the body with the knee bent at approximately 90 degrees.
The Cuban National Ballet, founded by Alicia Alonso, developed a distinctive style that blends Russian technique with Latin American flair.
Dancing en pointe was originally performed by men; women did not regularly dance on pointe until the early 1800s.
A jeté is any jump from one foot to the other, with the simplest being a small jeté and the most dramatic being a grand jeté.
The Dutch National Ballet, based in Amsterdam, is one of the largest ballet companies in the world.
Margot Fonteyn was born Peggy Hookham in Reigate, England, and adopted her stage name early in her career.
The barre is placed at a height of approximately 3.5 feet (106 cm), roughly at hip level for most adult dancers.
Capoeira is a Brazilian martial art that combines elements of dance, acrobatics, and music and was developed by enslaved Africans.
A pas de chat (step of the cat) is a light, springing jump where the dancer draws both feet up beneath them.
National Ballet of Canada, founded in 1951 by Celia Franca, is one of the premier ballet companies in North America.
Twyla Tharp created the dance sequences for the film Amadeus (1984) and Hair (1979).
The human foot contains 26 bones, 33 joints, and over 100 muscles, tendons, and ligaments—all of which a ballet dancer relies on intensely.
Sergei Diaghilev's Ballets Russes (1909–1929) revolutionized ballet by commissioning scores from Stravinsky, Prokofiev, and Debussy.
The first position in ballet has both heels touching with the feet turned out to roughly 180 degrees.
The movie Footloose (1984) featured a teenager who moves to a small town where dancing has been banned.
Vaslav Nijinsky was legendary for his ability to appear to hover in the air during jumps, a quality known as ballon.
A ballet class typically follows a structured format: barre work, center practice, adagio, allegro, and reverence.
The Pacific Northwest Ballet in Seattle premiered its acclaimed full-length Nutcracker with sets and costumes by Maurice Sendak.
Jerome Robbins choreographed Dances at a Gathering (1969), a plotless ballet set to Chopin piano music that is considered a masterpiece.
Kathak is a classical Indian dance form that shares some similarities with flamenco, including intricate footwork and spinning.
Male ballet dancers (danseurs) do not typically dance en pointe, though some modern choreographers have experimented with this.
A tour en l'air is a jump in which the male dancer completes one or more full rotations while airborne.
The Birmingham Royal Ballet is the UK's second major classical ballet company, touring extensively throughout Britain.
Agnes de Mille's choreography for the musical Oklahoma! (1943) was revolutionary for integrating dance into the storyline.
Professional ballet dancers may rehearse 6 to 8 hours per day during performance seasons.
The Charleston, a lively dance from the 1920s, became a symbol of the Jazz Age and was popularized by Josephine Baker.
Lincoln Kirstein co-founded the New York City Ballet with George Balanchine and was instrumental in establishing ballet in America.
Samba is a lively Brazilian dance performed during Carnival and is characterized by rapid hip movements and bouncing steps.
A sissonne is a jump from two feet landing on one foot, named after the Comte de Sissonne who invented it.
The Royal Danish Ballet, founded in 1748, is one of the oldest ballet companies in the world and preserves the Bournonville repertoire.
Akram Khan blends contemporary dance with kathak, creating a unique fusion that has won international acclaim.
Dancers often sew their own elastic and ribbons onto their pointe shoes, customizing the fit for their individual feet.
Balanchine's Apollo (1928) is considered the first neoclassical ballet and marked a turning point in 20th-century ballet.
A coda in ballet is the final section of a pas de deux where both the ballerina and her partner perform brilliant solo variations.
Bollywood dance blends classical Indian styles with modern Western dance and is a central feature of Indian cinema.
The Firebird, with music by Stravinsky and choreography by Michel Fokine, was the ballet that launched Diaghilev's Ballets Russes to fame.
Many ballet terms are in French because King Louis XIV formalized ballet in France and founded the Académie Royale de Danse in 1661.
A changement is a small jump starting and ending in fifth position where the feet change position in the air.
The Hamburg Ballet, under the direction of John Neumeier since 1973, is known for its narrative full-length ballets.
Krumping is an expressive, high-energy street dance that originated in the African American community of South Los Angeles.
During the Romantic era, gas lighting was first used in theaters, enhancing the ethereal quality of ballets like La Sylphide and Giselle.
World Ballet Day, an annual live-streamed event, features behind-the-scenes access to major ballet companies around the globe.
Fifth position, the most closed of the basic positions, has the feet fully crossed with the heel of each foot touching the toe of the other.
Crystal Pite is a Canadian choreographer known for her large-scale works that blend dance with theatrical elements.
The phrase 'break a leg' is commonly used as a good-luck wish in dance and theater because saying 'good luck' is considered bad luck.
The School of American Ballet, founded by Balanchine and Kirstein in 1934, is the official school of New York City Ballet.
The lindy hop, a swing dance, originated in Harlem in the late 1920s and was named after Charles Lindbergh's transatlantic flight.
Petipa's original production of The Sleeping Beauty in 1890 ran over four hours long.
An assemblé is a jump where the dancer brushes one foot along the floor, springs into the air, and lands on both feet.
The Royal Ballet, based at the Royal Opera House in Covent Garden, London, was founded in 1931 by Ninette de Valois.
Locking and popping are funk dance styles that originated in the 1970s and became essential elements of hip-hop dance culture.
Sylvie Guillem, known for her extraordinary flexibility, was the youngest étoile in Paris Opera Ballet history at age 19.
A ronds de jambe is a circular movement of the leg, either on the ground (à terre) or in the air (en l'air).
The Australian Ballet's production of Graeme Murphy's Swan Lake reimagines the classic story in a modern setting.
Paul Taylor, who danced with Martha Graham's company, went on to found his own influential modern dance company in 1954.
Natalia Makarova defected from the Kirov Ballet in 1970 and became a star at American Ballet Theatre.
An épaulement is the rotation of the shoulders and head in relation to the hips, adding artistry and dimension to ballet movements.
The jive, a fast-paced ballroom dance, evolved from the jitterbug and features kicks, flicks, and rock steps.
Diaghilev commissioned works from artists like Picasso, Matisse, and Coco Chanel for his revolutionary Ballets Russes productions.
A bourrée is a series of tiny, rapid steps performed on pointe or demi-pointe, creating an illusion of gliding across the stage.
The National Ballet of China, established in 1959, is known for its production of The Red Detachment of Women.
Salsa dancing originated in New York City in the 1960s and 1970s, blending Cuban Son, mambo, and other Latin dance styles.
Marie Camargo, an 18th-century ballerina, shortened her skirt to show her footwork, a revolutionary act at the time.
Port de bras (carriage of the arms) refers to the graceful movement and positioning of the arms in ballet.
Alicia Alonso, the legendary Cuban ballerina, continued to perform despite being legally blind for much of her career.
The Bolshoi Ballet's style is known for its dramatic expressiveness and powerful, expansive movements.
Wayne McGregor is the resident choreographer of the Royal Ballet and is known for his innovative use of technology in dance.
The foxtrot was introduced by vaudeville performer Harry Fox around 1914 and became one of the most popular ballroom dances.
A cabriole is a jump in which the extended legs are beaten together in the air before the dancer lands.
The Prix de Lausanne is an international ballet competition for young dancers aged 15 to 18 that serves as a gateway to professional careers.
In a fish dive, the ballerina is caught by her partner in a dramatic swooping position just above the floor.
The Martha Graham Dance Company was founded in 1926, making it one of the oldest dance companies in America.
Contra dance is a form of American folk dance where long lines of couples perform figures together, called by a caller.
Each pair of handmade Freed pointe shoes is stamped with the maker's personal mark, and top dancers request specific makers.
A manège is a circular path around the stage that dancers follow while performing turns or jumps, building excitement.
Ashley Bouder, a principal dancer with New York City Ballet, is known for her technical brilliance and joyful stage presence.
The Béjart Ballet Lausanne, founded by Maurice Béjart, is known for large-scale spectacles that blend ballet with avant-garde artistry.
Traditional ballet tutus come in two main styles: the short, stiff classical tutu and the longer, flowing Romantic tutu.
The Lindy Hop experienced a major revival in the 1980s and 1990s, with swing dance clubs opening worldwide.
George Balanchine died on April 30, 1983, leaving behind a legacy of over 400 ballets.
A dégagé is a movement where the working foot slides along the floor and lifts slightly off the ground.
The Vienna State Opera Ballet is one of Europe's oldest and most traditional ballet companies, known for its Viennese waltz expertise.
Mark Morris is an American choreographer known for his musicality and has been called 'the Mozart of modern dance.'
Ballerinas often break in new pointe shoes by bending, scoring, or even closing a door on them to soften the shank.
Giselle premiered at the Théâtre de l'Académie Royale de Musique in Paris in 1841.
Folk dance traditions exist in virtually every culture, from Greek sirtaki to Scottish Highland dancing to Hawaiian hula.
The Italian method of ballet, known as the Cecchetti method, places great emphasis on strength and balance.
A sauté is simply a jump from two feet landing on two feet, derived from the French word meaning 'to jump.'
The Dutch National Ballet's Junior Company provides young dancers with professional performance experience before joining the main company.
Alvin Ailey was born in Rogers, Texas, and drew on his African American heritage to create profoundly moving works.
The hora is a traditional circle dance performed at Jewish celebrations, especially weddings and bar mitzvahs.
A promenade in ballet is a slow turn in place while maintaining a position such as arabesque or attitude.
Wendy Whelan, a former New York City Ballet principal, was known for her long lines and her partnership with choreographer Christopher Wheeldon.
The history of pointe work dates back to the early 1800s, with Italian ballerina Amalia Brugnoli among the first to dance on her toes.
A frappé ('struck') is an exercise at the barre where the ball of the foot strikes the floor with a sharp, quick motion.
Swan Lake features a dual role: the White Swan (Odette) and the Black Swan (Odile), often danced by the same ballerina.
The Scottish Ballet, founded in 1969, is Scotland's national dance company and is based in Glasgow.
Butoh is an avant-garde Japanese dance form characterized by slow, controlled movements and often white body paint.
Ninette de Valois, born Edris Stannus in Ireland, founded what became the Royal Ballet and the Royal Ballet School.
A variation (solo) in classical ballet showcases an individual dancer's technique, artistry, and interpretation.
The tango argentino is improvised and more intimate than the ballroom tango, with a focus on connection between partners.
Yuri Grigorovich served as ballet master and chief choreographer of the Bolshoi Ballet for over 30 years.
The Vaganova Academy in St. Petersburg accepts students at age 10 after rigorous auditions assessing physique, musicality, and potential.
A balancé is a waltz step that shifts weight from one foot to the other with a gentle rocking motion.
Les Sylphides, choreographed by Michel Fokine in 1909, is a plotless, atmospheric ballet set to the music of Chopin.
Bollywood choreographer Farah Khan has created dance sequences for over 100 Hindi films.
Professional male ballet dancers can lift their partners overhead hundreds of times during a single full-length ballet.
Classical Indian dance includes eight recognized forms: Bharatanatyam, Kathak, Kathakali, Kuchipudi, Manipuri, Mohiniyattam, Odissi, and Sattriya.
Peter Martins served as ballet master in chief of New York City Ballet from 1990 to 2018.
The quickstep is a fast-paced ballroom dance that evolved from a combination of the foxtrot, Charleston, and other dances in the 1920s.
A soubresaut is a jump from fifth position where the body travels through the air in a straight line without changing the position of the feet.
Gelsey Kirkland was one of the most celebrated American ballerinas of the 1970s, famous for her roles in Giselle and The Nutcracker.
Bharatanatyam is one of the oldest classical dance forms, originating in the temples of Tamil Nadu, India, over 2,000 years ago.
The studio mirror is an essential tool for dancers, allowing them to self-correct alignment, placement, and form during practice.
Don Quixote premiered in its original version at the Bolshoi Theatre in Moscow.
Christopher Wheeldon won a Tony Award for his choreography of An American in Paris on Broadway in 2015.
A chaîné turn is a series of rapid half-turns performed while traveling across the floor.
The Polish National Ballet, based at the Grand Theatre in Warsaw, is one of the largest ballet companies in Europe.
Vogue dance originated in the Harlem ballroom scene of the 1960s and was popularized by Madonna's 1990 hit 'Vogue.'
Dance notation systems like Labanotation and Benesh Movement Notation allow choreography to be recorded on paper like a musical score.
Male dancers who partner ballerinas must develop tremendous upper body strength while maintaining graceful, effortless-looking technique.
The Rite of Spring, with its pounding rhythms and angular movements, marked a dramatic departure from traditional ballet.
An allegro section in ballet class features fast, energetic movements including small and large jumps.
The Royal Winnipeg Ballet, founded in 1939, is the oldest continuously operating ballet company in Canada.
Loïe Fuller pioneered the use of theatrical lighting and silk costumes to create mesmerizing visual effects in her performances.
Pointe shoes have a stiff box at the front (made of layers of fabric, paste, and paper) that supports the dancer's toes.
The shank of a pointe shoe provides arch support and varies in stiffness depending on the dancer's strength and preference.
La Sylphide (1832), choreographed by Filippo Taglioni for his daughter Marie, is the oldest surviving Romantic ballet.
The mazurka is a Polish folk dance in triple time that was incorporated into many 19th-century ballets.
The Alvin Ailey School in New York trains approximately 3,500 students annually in dance.
A pesté is a small, percussive step where the foot is placed sharply on the floor with a stamping motion.
ABT's Met season at the Metropolitan Opera House in New York is one of the most prestigious annual ballet events in America.
Strictly Come Dancing has been a British television institution since 2004, pairing celebrities with professional dancers.
Sergei Prokofiev composed the ballet scores for Romeo and Juliet and Cinderella, both masterworks of the 20th-century repertoire.
Erik Bruhn, a Danish dancer often called the finest danseur noble of his generation, was a mentor to Rudolf Nureyev.
A temps lié is a flowing exercise that teaches smooth weight transfers and coordination of arms, legs, and head.
The Finnish National Ballet, founded in 1922, performs at the Finnish National Opera and Ballet in Helsinki.
The moonwalk, made famous by Michael Jackson in 1983, is actually a variation of a glide step used by mime artists and dancers for decades.
In the Vaganova method, students train for eight years before graduating and joining a professional company.
A révérence is the formal bow or curtsy performed at the end of a ballet class as a mark of respect and gratitude.
The Pennsylvania Ballet merged with Philadelphia's BalletX in spirit of collaboration, keeping Philadelphia on the ballet map.
The can-can, a high-energy dance featuring high kicks and splits, became famous at the Moulin Rouge in Paris in the 1840s.
Darcey Bussell, one of Britain's most famous ballerinas, became the youngest principal dancer at the Royal Ballet at age 20.
A posé turn begins with a step onto a straight leg into relevé, followed by a full rotation.
The Boston Ballet, founded in 1963, is the fourth-largest professional ballet company in the United States.
Matthew Bourne's all-male Swan Lake (1995) reimagined the roles of the swans with male dancers, winning an Olivier Award.
In classical ballet, the ballerina's hair is traditionally worn in a neat bun to maintain clean lines and prevent distraction.
Arthur Mitchell, the first Black principal dancer at New York City Ballet, founded the Dance Theatre of Harlem in 1969.
The waltz originated in Austria and southern Germany in the late 18th century.
A coupé is a position where one foot is placed at the ankle of the supporting leg, either front or back.
The Zurich Ballet is one of Europe's leading companies and has been directed by Christian Spuck since 2012.
Benjamin Millepied, who choreographed Black Swan, was appointed director of dance at the Paris Opera Ballet in 2014.
During the Romantic era, the tutu was lengthened to calf-length to create a more ethereal, mysterious atmosphere on stage.
Galina Ulanova, considered one of the greatest ballerinas of the 20th century, danced with the Bolshoi Ballet for 16 years.
Ballet slippers (soft shoes) are typically made of leather, canvas, or satin and are worn for non-pointe work.
The rumba, known as the 'dance of love,' is a slow, sensual ballroom dance of Cuban origin.
Ohad Naharin developed Gaga, a movement language used by Batsheva Dance Company that focuses on sensation and improvisation.
A failli is a movement where the dancer jumps through first position in the air, landing with one foot in front.
The National Ballet of Japan, established in 1997, performs at the New National Theatre in Tokyo.
The original choreography for many classical ballets has been lost and reconstructed from notation, photographs, and oral tradition.
Swan Lake's iconic opening theme, played by the oboe, is one of the most recognizable melodies in classical music.
Male ballet dancers' jumps can reach heights of over two feet and cover distances of more than 10 feet.
A sous-sus is a relevé in fifth position where the feet are tightly crossed with no visible space between them.
The Hungarian National Ballet is based in Budapest and performs at the Hungarian State Opera House.
Anne Teresa De Keersmaeker is a Belgian choreographer known for her rigorous, mathematically structured dances set to minimalist music.
The jitterbug emerged in the 1930s alongside swing music and evolved into various partner dances including the Lindy Hop.
In the 18th century, male dancers dominated ballet; the rise of the ballerina came during the Romantic era in the 1830s.
A batterie refers to any movement in which the legs beat together during a jump.
The Norwegian National Ballet, based in Oslo, is Norway's largest dance company.
Jiří Kylián served as artistic director of the Nederlands Dans Theater for over 20 years, creating over 100 works.
Balanchine developed the neoclassical style, stripping away elaborate costumes and stories to focus on pure movement and musicality.
The bambuco is the national dance of Colombia and combines European and indigenous influences.
Benjamin Britten composed the music for The Prince of the Pagodas (1957), the first full-length ballet score by a British composer.
The term 'ballerina' technically refers to a principal female dancer; other ranks include soloist, coryphée, and corps member.
Mikhail Fokine's Les Sylphides revolutionized ballet by emphasizing mood and atmosphere over plot.
A grand allegro is the most physically demanding section of a ballet class, featuring large traveling jumps across the floor.
The Cincinnati Ballet, founded in 1963, serves as both a classical and contemporary dance company.
The Charleston dance craze swept through the United States in the 1920s after appearing in the Broadway show Runnin' Wild.
Dancers sometimes apply jet glue or shellac to the tips of pointe shoes to extend their lifespan on hard stages.
The rosin box, found in every ballet studio and backstage area, prevents dancers from slipping.
Sergei Diaghilev was born in Novgorod, Russia, and became the most influential impresario in ballet history.
A piqué turn begins with a step directly onto pointe or demi-pointe on a straight leg, immediately followed by a turn.
The Basel Ballet in Switzerland is known for its contemporary repertoire and innovative programming.
Igor Moiseyev founded the Moiseyev Ballet in 1937, the first professional folk dance company in the Soviet Union.
Dancers develop incredibly strong feet; a ballerina's foot strength can rival that of a gymnast's grip strength.
Stravinsky's Petrushka, about three puppets at a Russian fair, premiered in 1911.
A ballon refers to the ability of a dancer to appear to float in the air during jumps before landing softly.
The Korean National Ballet, founded in 1962, is based in Seoul and performs both classical and contemporary works.
Alexei Ratmansky is one of the most sought-after choreographers in the world and has worked with ABT, the Bolshoi, and many others.
The phrase 'prima ballerina assoluta' is the highest rank a female dancer can receive and has been awarded to very few in history.
Limbering and stretching exercises are essential parts of a dancer's daily routine to maintain flexibility and prevent injury.
Mats Ek reimagined classical ballets like Giselle and Swan Lake through a contemporary lens, often with surprising and provocative imagery.
Nutcracker performances account for nearly 40% of many American ballet companies' annual ticket revenue.
The floor of a ballet studio is typically made of sprung wood to absorb impact and reduce injuries.
World's oldest ballet, Le Ballet comique de la Reine, was performed in 1581 at the French court.
The Perm Ballet, also known as the Tchaikovsky Academic Theatre, is one of Russia's oldest ballet companies.
An echappé is a jump or relevé from fifth position in which the feet open to second or fourth position and close back to fifth.
The annual Youth America Grand Prix is the world's largest student ballet competition, with participants from over 30 countries.
Frederic Franklin, a legendary British-American dancer and ballet master, performed and taught well into his 90s.
The mazurka step is characterized by stamping and heel-clicking, reflecting its origin as a Polish folk dance.
Alexandra Danilova was a beloved Russian ballerina who became a star with Balanchine's early companies in Europe and America.
A gargouillade is a complex step involving a circular movement of both legs during a jump.
The Suzanne Farrell Ballet, founded by Balanchine's last muse, was dedicated to performing and preserving his works.
Dancers' feet undergo significant physical changes over a career, including thickened skin, bunions, and shortened toenails.
George Balanchine's The Nutcracker, first performed by NYCB in 1954, established the American tradition of annual Nutcracker productions.
The rumba walk is a basic movement in ballroom dancing that emphasizes hip motion through the settling of weight on each step.
John Cranko's Onegin, based on Pushkin's verse novel, is a dramatic ballet that remains a staple of the international repertoire.
A brisé is a small beating jump that travels, where the legs beat together in the air.
The London Festival Ballet, now English National Ballet, was founded to make ballet accessible to wider audiences.
Traditional Chinese dance includes classical, folk, and ethnic styles, each reflecting different regions and cultural traditions.
The ballet master or ballet mistress is responsible for rehearsing the company, maintaining standards, and coaching dancers in their roles.
Maya Plisetskaya, the Bolshoi's greatest star, was known for her extraordinary extensions and her legendary Carmen Suite.
A penché is an extreme arabesque where the dancer tips forward, raising the back leg until it approaches a vertical split.
The Joffrey Ballet relocated from New York to Chicago in 1995 and became the premier ballet company of the Midwest.
Lester Horton developed a modern dance technique that emphasizes flat backs, lateral stretches, and deep lunges.
Igor Stravinsky's three early ballet scores—The Firebird, Petrushka, and The Rite of Spring—transformed both ballet and music.
A rond de jambe en l'air involves circular movements of the lower leg while the thigh remains still at hip height.
The Cape Town City Ballet is the oldest ballet company in South Africa, founded in 1934.
Justin Peck became New York City Ballet's resident choreographer in 2014 at age 27, the youngest in the company's history.
The term 'tutu' likely derives from the French children's word 'cucu,' referring to the bottom, as the short skirt reveals the legs.
Mikhail Baryshnikov hosted Saturday Night Live in 1978 and appeared in the TV series Sex and the City as a character named Aleksandr Petrovsky.
A temps levé is a hop on one foot, one of the simplest forms of jump in ballet.
Dance therapy, also known as dance/movement therapy, uses movement to support emotional, cognitive, and physical integration.
Vaslav Nijinsky choreographed L'Après-midi d'un faune in 1912, a ballet that caused controversy for its sensual final gesture.
An enveloppé is the reverse of a développé, where the extended leg is drawn back to the knee before closing.
The Miami City Ballet, founded in 1985 by Edward Villella, specializes in Balanchine's works.
Stomp, the percussion show that uses everyday objects as instruments, blurs the line between dance and percussion performance.
Cross-training activities like Pilates, yoga, and swimming are commonly used by ballet dancers to prevent injuries and build strength.
The Korean dance form talchum combines masked dance, drama, and satire and has been performed for centuries.
Second position has the feet turned out and separated by roughly the width of the dancer's shoulders.
Daniil Simkin is known for his explosive jumps and turns and has performed as a principal dancer with ABT.
The ballet Jewels (1967) by Balanchine is considered the first full-length plotless ballet, with sections set to Fauré, Stravinsky, and Tchaikovsky.
Copeland published her memoir 'Life in Motion' in 2014, detailing her journey from a late start in dance to becoming a ballet star.
A ballotté is a rocking jump where the body alternately tilts forward and backward, creating a see-saw effect.
The Semperoper Ballett in Dresden is one of Germany's top ballet companies.
Tap dance shoes have metal plates on the toe and heel to create percussive rhythms with each step.
The oldest known dance notation system, Feuillet notation, was published by Raoul-Auger Feuillet in 1700.
The Kirov Academy of Ballet in Washington, D.C. is modeled on the Vaganova system and trains students from around the world.
A sissonne ouverte lands on one foot with the other leg extended in the air, creating an open position.
Marie Taglioni's father, Filippo Taglioni, was the choreographer of La Sylphide and trained her rigorously from childhood.
The Bangarra Dance Theatre in Australia blends Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander traditions with contemporary dance.
Carlos Acosta retired from classical ballet in 2015 and founded Acosta Danza, a Cuban dance company.
Rudolf Nureyev served as director of dance at the Paris Opera Ballet from 1983 to 1989.
Margot Fonteyn continued performing well into her 50s, an extraordinary longevity for a ballerina.
A emboîté is a series of small, alternating jumps where the feet are drawn up underneath the body.
Igor Zelensky, a former Kirov and NYCB star, served as artistic director of the Bavarian State Ballet.
The paso doble is a dramatic ballroom dance that mimics the movements of a Spanish bullfight.
Third position, rarely used in professional ballet, places one foot in front of the other with the heel at the arch.
The ballet Romeo and Juliet by Prokofiev was rejected by the Bolshoi Theatre before its Czechoslovak premiere in 1938.
A soutenu turn begins by drawing one foot to the other while turning, ending in fifth position on relevé.
The Harlem Shake dance trend of 2013 was unrelated to the original Harlem Shake, a hip-hop dance from the 1980s.
Dancers use lamb's wool, gel pads, or paper towels inside their pointe shoes to cushion their toes.
The Polish mazurka influenced many classical ballet variations, particularly those in Coppélia and Swan Lake.
Wendy Whelan performed her farewell concert at New York City Ballet in 2014 after 30 years with the company.
Ballet requires extreme hip flexibility; most professional dancers can achieve at least 180 degrees of turnout from the hips.
The Kirov (Mariinsky) Theatre was originally built in 1860 and remains one of the most beautiful opera houses in the world.
The Brazilian zouk is a partner dance characterized by flowing movements and deep dips.
Roland Petit was a French choreographer known for his theatrical ballets, including Carmen and Le Jeune Homme et la Mort.
A détourné is a turn in place that changes the position of the feet without leaving the floor.
The ABT Jacqueline Kennedy Onassis School is American Ballet Theatre's official training academy in New York City.
Ghanaian dancer and choreographer Germaine Acogny is considered the mother of contemporary African dance.
The iconic 32 fouetté turns in Swan Lake Act III are among the most anticipated moments in all of ballet.
Ballet dancers lose an average of one to two liters of sweat per performance.
The word 'choreography' comes from the Greek words 'choreia' (dance) and 'grapho' (to write).
National Dance Day in the United States was established by congressional resolution and is celebrated in late July.
